strict_pre_incident.rationale: Activity context shapes where a safety team may search; spearfishing is context, not automatic provocation.
Recent public fatal/nonfatal interactions near the mission area increase search priority.
Verified public sighting reports near the mission area increase search priority.
Reef, dropoff, channel, or habitat features can help define useful drone search zones.
River mouths, inlets, and runoff zones may deserve targeted search attention where regionally relevant.
Activity hazard feeds surveillance priority separately from environmental warning score.

drone_restriction_notes.routine_drone_monitoring: limited by Sydney Airport flight-path restrictions
drone_restriction_notes.operational_boundary: human-approved surveillance only; no autonomous flight and no bypassing aviation restrictions

drone_restriction_context: Sydney Airport flight-path constraints / emergency review context
operational_framing: Authorities increased drone and helicopter surveillance after the incident, demonstrating that aerial observation is already part of the practical coastal response. AI1SAD should support this by producing structured surveillance priorities, recommended patrol patterns, and map-ready observation feeds for human-operated aerial monitoring.

post_incident_media_evidence.aerial_video_source: ABC News / social media aerial video as reported by ABC
post_incident_media_evidence.same_individual_note: footage indicates a white shark in the area after the attack, but authorities cautioned this does not prove it was the same shark involved
post_incident_media_evidence.possible_blood_plume_note: analyst/user visual observation only; do not treat as confirmed source fact
post_incident_media_evidence.public_review_summary: Aerial footage after the incident showed a large shark in the area; source reporting indicates probable white shark, but the same-individual link remains unconfirmed.
